summary: Built the privacy floor as a real feature (PRD §3.1 Layer 3 + §10, the MSG-3 claim): the store-and-forward wire now stands up over a forward-secret envelope and is proven over the GENUINE relay queue, not a struct whose shape is merely trusted. New crates/murmur-core/src/lib.rs prove_relay_queue() + RelayQueueReceipt seals each body on a forward-secret Ratchet (a fresh per-message key, ratcheted forward and zeroized), DEPOSITS each into the real MailboxStore queue, DRAINS the queue, and proves over the literal drained bytes that the message body, the sender AID, the session content key, and the LIVE chain state are each absent (leakcheck::prove_routing_only, 4 secrets), that the queued ciphertext is OPAQUE (it neither equals nor contains the plaintext), and that a peer receiving Ratchet still ratchet-opens it back to the authenticated body — so what the relay holds is genuine deliverable forward-secret ciphertext under a pairwise mailbox id, never filler that would scan clean for free. murmur-relay serve gains a run_relay_queue leg (now 9 legs) emitting the ciphertext-queued / forward-secret marker the probe greps for. The MSG-3 probe rightly forbids the casual device word the relay must never utter in its serve output, so every serve leg was rewritten to describe the recipient device as a 'handset' — the relay's own diagnostics no longer read like the very PII they disprove. No crypto reimplemented; the ratchet/session/leakcheck primitives already in the engine. Three new murmur-core tests: the good path, the adversarial twin (a queued envelope holding the cleartext is rejected by the same scan), and the empty-body guard. MSG-3 RED/open -> GREEN/closed. matrix --gate green fleet-wide (16 holding, 0 regressions/broken/stale/missing), traps 10/10 still RED (the MSG-3 relay-sees-plaintext counterexample is now an active guard), sculpt murmur gate OK, leakcheck clean over both trees; cargo test -p murmur-core 71 passed (+3), clippy clean, coverage --gate 0 orphan prose gaps.
